items.headline: U.S. Stocks Extend Gains on Strong Earnings, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit Record Highs
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are largely higher today, extending April's record-setting momentum as strong quarterly earnings, particularly from major technology companies, fuel investor optimism. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both reached new intraday and closing highs. Despite the positive sentiment, some market commentators express caution regarding valuations and potential divergences from economic reality. The rally appears concentrated in a handful of mega-cap stocks. Concerns around the ongoing Middle East conflict and its impact on oil prices and inflation continue to be monitored.

items.statContext: +0.64% Today, extending gains after closing at an all-time high on Thursday.
+1.07% Today, reaching new intraday and closing highs.

items.headline: Apple (AAPL) Jumps on Strong iPhone Demand
items.summary: Apple Inc. ($AAPL) shares climbed 4.46% during midday trading to a reported price of $185.50 after the company exceeded Wall Street's second-quarter earnings expectations, driven by "extraordinary" demand for its latest iPhone 17 lineup.

items.headline: Estée Lauder (EL) Surges on Upbeat Outlook
items.summary: Estée Lauder Companies Inc. ($EL) surged 12% in premarket trading, extending gains after the cosmetics giant posted third-quarter results that surpassed analyst expectations and subsequently raised its full-year financial outlook.

items.headline: Atlassian (TEAM) Soars on Raised Forecast
items.summary: Atlassian Corporation Plc ($TEAM) saw its shares surge 23.68% during early trading, reaching $84.83, after the enterprise software maker lifted its annual forecast, signaling strong performance.

items.headline: Smart Powerr Corp (CREG) Plummets
items.summary: Smart Powerr Corp. ($CREG) experienced a significant decline today, with shares falling 41.26% to $0.520.

items.headline: Sadot Group Inc. (SDOT) Sees Steep Drop
items.summary: Sadot Group Inc. ($SDOT) shares dropped 34.47% to $0.639.

items.headline: HUB Cyber Security (HUBC) Falls Sharply
items.summary: HUB Cyber Security Ltd. ($HUBC) shares decreased by 34.33% to $0.541.

items.headline: Roblox (RBLX) Tumbles on Revenue Miss and Outlook Cut
items.summary: Roblox Corporation ($RBLX) saw its shares tumble 15.95% to $46.43 after the video-game company missed revenue estimates and slashed its annual bookings forecast. The company cited child-protection safeguards impacting user numbers.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady Amid Dissent and Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained its benchmark interest rate in the range of 3.5% to 3.75% following its latest meeting. The decision, however, was met with an unusually high four dissents from FOMC members, with some citing ongoing uncertainty regarding inflation and downside risks to the job market, particularly due to rising oil prices. Fed Chair Jerome Powell's term concludes on May 15, 2026.

items.headline: U.S. Q1 GDP Expands 2%, Driven by Business Investment in AI
items.summary: The U.S. economy grew at an annualized rate of 2% in the first quarter of 2026, a notable increase from the 0.5% growth in the last three months of 2025. Business investment, particularly in AI data center construction, was a primary driver, contributing 1.39 percentage points to the headline figure. Conversely, consumer spending decelerated, suggesting a reliance on savings.

items.headline: Tech Sector Leads Market as AI Hyperscalers Report Strong Earnings
items.summary: Technology stocks are leading today's market rally, bolstered by robust earnings reports from major AI 'hyperscaler' companies including Alphabet ($GOOG), Amazon ($AMZN), Microsoft ($MSFT), and Apple ($AAPL). These companies generally exceeded revenue and profit estimates, especially in cloud computing and consumer hardware, reinforcing the federal AI spending narrative with new Pentagon deals. Nvidia ($NVDA) and Alphabet are currently vying for the title of the world's most valuable company.

items.headline: Oil Giants Exxon Mobil (XOM) and Chevron (CVX) Beat Earnings, Profits Decline Amid High Oil Prices
items.summary: Energy majors Exxon Mobil ($XOM) and Chevron ($CVX) both reported first-quarter earnings that surpassed Wall Street expectations. However, both companies experienced a drop in profits, which they attributed to surging oil prices stemming from the ongoing Middle East conflict. Oil prices have soared since the conflict began in February, leading to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: May Day Protests Call for Economic Blackout Amid Rising Costs
items.summary: Thousands across the U.S. are participating in 'May Day Strong' protests today, calling for an 'economic blackout' with calls for 'no school, no work, no shopping.' Organized demonstrations are demanding higher wages, better working conditions, and an end to the Iran war, which activists link to rising energy costs and shrinking purchasing power.
